notableMoments: Iraq's only previous World Cup appearance was in 1986 in Mexico, where they lost all three group matches against Paraguay, Belgium, and hosts Mexico
Ahmed Radhi scored Iraq's first and only World Cup goal in 1986 against Belgium, a moment of national pride that is still celebrated in Iraqi football
Won the 2007 Asian Cup in a remarkable underdog run during a period of devastating conflict, uniting the nation in one of sport's greatest feel-good stories
The 2026 World Cup qualification marks a 40-year wait to return to football's biggest stage, making it one of the most emotionally charged qualifications in the tournament
Qualified through the intercontinental playoff pathway, demonstrating resilience and determination that mirrors the Iraqi people's spirit
Iraq's football has persevered through decades of conflict and sanctions, making every national team achievement a symbol of hope and unity for the country
The current squad features a blend of players from the Iraqi Premier League and Gulf-based leagues, led by prolific striker Aymen Hussein and talented young forward Mohanad Ali
Iraqi football fans are among the most passionate in Asia, with the national team's matches regularly drawing massive crowds and emotional celebrations across the country
